content: Just as linguistic reflection (the reflexion concept from high school language learning) requires stepping back to examine how one’s own word choices shape meaning, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by borrowing the structured metacognitive practice of language reflection writing workshops to anchor inconsistent self-evaluation perspectives. This framework turns unstructured LLM self-critique into a guided, iterative writing-like exercise that mirrors how human learners refine their reasoning through intentional reflective journaling.
